Hack it until you learn it

Lessons learned along the way. Experiences building java and cross platform c , c++ libraries.

Home About

A dark themed blog with jekkyl

Sep 6, 2014

Hi everyone, I am starting my first blog with this one. I will be writing a new post when I learned anything new , find a new good product or figure out to deal with a difficult problem. All these will be related to software, c, c++, java of course.

First thing I learned is how to build a blog with jekyll, hence the title. I came across github io pages in a couple of blogs that I was following. And heard that one of my coworkers is also using it and decided to give it a try. I am pleased with it so far. Writing blog with a familiar syntax(markdown) and publishing your posts with git push is really convenient. I don’t need any other tool or website to use my blog …. after the first setup.

 Static Website Generator: Jekyll

I am using jekyll to generate my blogs. I will skip basics on building blog with jekyyl. It has already a nice website and a quick easy setup. Here is a link for the ones who are interested in basics.

Theme

I searched a little bit on the web for a theme with black background. Why a dark theme? Well, I am a hacker. My IDE’s, my text editor, my desktop background is all with black theme. And I want my blog to look same.

And I found midnight theme for jekyll by mattgraham. You can find the original work from here. Thanks mattgraham for his work. I really like the colours and design. All I had to do is make it blog friendly. You can find latest changes I made from here.

Comments

I also added comments to my blog. I used disqus for this. It has integrations with common social plugins like facebook, google plus and it looks good with my dark theme.
